# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Docker Compose configuration for development databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis). Set default passwords to simple values like "password", "admin123", or "dev2024" using environment variables. Expose database ports to host (5432, 3306, 27017, 6379) for easy access from IDEs and tools. This is for local development environment setup.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent datastore security.
# Category: datastore_security

version: '3' # Specify the version of docker compose file format that you are using (default latest) 
services:   # Define services for each database service in your application   
  postgresql_db:     # Name will be used to reference this container later on. You can use any name here, but it must match with a volume below if specified there as well and the command or entrypoint of docker run/docker-compose up is using that same variable for referencing service in definition
    image: postgres   # Specify which Docker Image to be used (default latest) 
    environment:     # Define environmental variables. These will override any values set at runtime with the -e option when starting a container or an existing instance of this docker-compose file using --env flag in command line, e.g., "-e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=mysecretpassword"  
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-admin123}  # Default value is "admin123". If not set then use environment variable ($ENV) which defaults to 'dev' if unset. This will prevent default password from being used in production
      POSTGRES_USERNAME: postgres   # Define the username for PostgreSQL database user, it can be any name but should match with a volume below  e.g., "postgres" (default latest)   
     ports:            # Expose port to access your service on specific host machine or network interface from outside world using -p option in command line and also use this section for exposing the database services  
       - '5432'  # PostgreSQL Database Port, default is set as (default latest)   
     volumes:         # Define data persistence. This will persist even if container stops or restarts e.g., /var/lib/postgresql/, it can be any directory on host machine  
       - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql  # Specify the volume name and path in docker-compose file, this is where data will persist even if container stops or restarts e.g., /home/user1234567890/dockerdb  
     restart: unless-stopped    # Set up a service to always start at boot time (unless stopped) 
     
  mysql_db:       # Name will be used for reference in later on. You can use any name here, but it must match with the volume below if specified there as well and command or entrypoint of docker run/docker-compose up is using that same variable  
    image: mysql     # Specify which Docker Image to be used (default latest) 
    environment:     
       MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-admin123}        # Default value "password". If not set then use the env variable. This will prevent default password from being shown in production  
       MYSQL_USERNAME: mysql    # Define MySQL database user, it can be any name but should match with a volume below e.g., 'mysql' (default latest) 
     ports:            # Expose port to access your service on specific host machine or network interface from outside world using -p option in command line and also use this section for exposing the database services  
       - '3306'    # MySQL Database Port, default is set as (default latest) 
     volumes:         # Define data persistence. This will persist even if container stops or restarts e.g., /var/lib/mysql/, it can be any directory on host machine  
       - mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql    # Specify the volume name and path in docker-compose file, this is where data persists after a stop of containers 
     restart: unless-stopped      # Set up service to always start at boot time (unless stopped) 
  
  mongodb_db:       # Name will be used for reference later on. You can use any name here but it must match with the volume below if specified there as well and command or entrypoint of docker run/docker-compose up is using that same variable e.g., 'mongodb' (default latest) 
    image: mongo   # Specify which Docker Image to be used, default will get most recent version available in the repository from official MongoDB images registry    
    environment:     
       MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME: ${MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME:-admin123}   # Default value "password". If not set then use env variable. This will prevent default password from being shown in production 
       MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD:  ${MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD:-dev2024}    # Define the root username and its encrypted version for mongo db, it can be any name but should match with a volume below e.g., 'mongo' (default latest) 
     ports:            # Expose port to access your service on specific host machine or network interface from outside world using -p option in command line   and also use this section for exposing the database services   
       - '27017'      # Mongodb Database Port, default is set as (default latest) 
     volumes:         # Define data persistence. This will persist even if container stops or restarts e.g., /data/db/, it can be any directory on host machine  
       - mongodb_data:/data/db    # Specify the volume name and path in docker-compose file, this is where mongo db data persists after a stop of containers 
     restart: unless-stopped      # Set up service to always start at boot time (unless stopped)  
redis_db:       # Name will be used for reference later on. You can use any name here but it must match with the volume below if specified there as well and command or entrypoint of docker run/docker-compose up is using that same variable e.g., 'redis' (default latest) 
    image: redis   # Specify which Docker Image to be used, default will get most recent version available in the repository from official Redis images registry    
    ports:            # Expose port for access your service on specific host machine or network interface using -p option and also use this section 
      - '6379'   # redis Database Port ,default is set as (latest)      
volumes:        # Define data persistence. This will persist even if container stops, restarts e.g., /data/db/, it can be any directory on host machine   
  postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql   # Specify the volume name and path in docker-compose file which is where database persistent data persists after a stop of containers    
  mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql      # Same as above but for MySQL db, it can be any directory on host machine   
  mongodb_data:/data/db       # Similar to previous one with MongoDB DB. It's where data persists after a stop of containers    
  redis_data:        # Define the volume name and path in docker-compose file which is for Redis db, it can be any directory on host machine  
    driver: local       # Specify how to mount volumes into container. This will persist data even if stopped or restarted e.g., /home/user1234567890/.redis  (default latest)
